1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (Iter-122: per the iter-121 user pivot directive, the project
6	operates as a Mathlib contributor; this iter executes the first
7	M1-route prover lane after iter-121's strategic-pivot setup.)
8	
9	The iter-122 strategy-critic CHALLENGED M1's place on the critical
10	path; the planner accepted the challenge in STRATEGY.md § M1 (reframed
11	as "OFF the critical path; on Mathlib-contributor track per user
12	directive"), and additionally scheduled the iter-123 M3 route-pick
13	audit + the iter-124+ M2.a Rigidity refactor. The iter-122 prover lane
14	proceeds as planned per the iter-121 fallback (`Continue executing the
15	M1 roadmap as named in STRATEGY.md`).
16	
17	## Stages
18	- [x] init
19	- [x] autoformalize
20	- [ ] prover
21	- [ ] polish
22	
23	## End-state overview
24	
25	**Zero inline `sorry`** in the project. Per the revised STRATEGY.md:

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
69	
70	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
71	
72	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
73	
74	/-- The submonoid of "good" elements in `Γ(S, U)`: those whose image under the
75	appLE algebra map `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` induced by `f : X ⟶ S` is a unit in
76	`Γ(X, V)`. This is the multiplicative set at which `Γ(S, U) → A_colim` is a
77	localization (cf. `appLE_isLocalization`). -/
78	def appLE_unitSubmonoid (f : X ⟶ S)
79	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
80	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Submonoid Γ(S, U) where
81	  carrier := { g | IsUnit ((Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom g) }
82	  one_mem' := by
83	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_one]
84	    exact isUnit_one
85	  mul_mem' := by
86	    intro a b ha hb
87	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_mul] at ha hb ⊢
88	    exact ha.mul hb
89	
90	/-- The inverse-image presheaf colimit ring at `V` is the localization
91	of `Γ(S, U)` at the submonoid `appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e`.
92	
93	This is milestone M1.b of the project roadmap: the heart of the bridge between
94	the section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` and the appLE-algebra
95	Kähler module on an affine chart. The proof goes via the two-direction
96	`IsLocalization.of_le` pattern (see
97	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge`).
98	
99	The canonical `Algebra Γ(S, U) A_colim` structure is the cocone leg of
100	the directed colimit at the open `U` (valid since `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` means
101	`fV ⊆ U`, so `U` is in the index category of the colimit). Constructing
102	this algebra structure rigorously is part of the prover's M1.b task; in
103	the stub we introduce it via a `sorry`-ed `letI`. -/
104	theorem appLE_isLocalization (f : X ⟶ S)
105	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
106	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
107	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U)
108	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) :=
109	      sorry
110	    IsLocalization (appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e)
111	      (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) := by
112	  sorry
113	
114	end AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
115	
116	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
117	
118	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
119	
120	/-- The bridge between the presheaf form and the algebra-Kähler form of the
121	relative cotangent module on an affine chart (milestone M1 of the project
122	roadmap). The section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over an
123	affine `V ⊆ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` is `Γ(X, V)`-linearly equivalent to the appLE-algebra
124	Kähler module `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)`.
125	
126	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge` for the proof
127	strategy (M1.a–M1.e).
128	
129	The `Module Γ(X, V)` instance on the LHS section module is supplied by the
130	`PresheafOfModules` structure (via the underlying
131	`(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).obj (.op V) : ModuleCat _`); in the
132	stub we introduce it via a `sorry`-ed `letI` so the type checks, and the
133	prover will replace this with the canonical transport-of-instance when
134	they refine the construction. -/
135	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE
136	    (f : X ⟶ S)
137	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
138	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
139	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
140	      (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
141	    letI : Module Γ(X, V) ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)) :=
142	      sorry
143	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)) ≃ₗ[Γ(X, V)]
144	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e) := by
145	  sorry
146	
147	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
148	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
149	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
150	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
151	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
152	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
153	
154	The bridge from this algebra-Kähler form to the project's presheaf
155	form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)` is a
156	Mathlib gap: the section module of the presheaf identifies with the
157	Kähler module over the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring
158	`colim_{f V ⊆ W ⊆ S} Γ(S, W)`, which is a localization of `Γ(S, U)`
159	and hence yields an iso `Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ≃ Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ A_colim]`
160	via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`; constructing the iso
161	requires presheaf-level cofinality machinery (~200–400 LOC) that is
162	out of autonomous-loop scope. See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`
163	§ "Bridge to the relative cotangent presheaf — out of autonomous-loop
164	scope" for the mathematical content.
165	
166	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
167	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
168	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
169	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
170	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
171	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
172	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
173	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
174	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
175	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
176	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
177	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
178	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
179	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
180	  intro x
181	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
182	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
183	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
184	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
185	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
186	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
187	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
188	      first
189	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
190	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
191	
192	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$, where $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}$ denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies that on every affine chart $V \to U$ (with $V \subseteq X$, $U \subseteq S$ affine and $f V \subseteq U$) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The relationship between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ is the content of milestone~M1 of the project roadmap and is stated as Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE} in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}$ on $X$ (the left Kan extension of $\struct{S}$ along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$, given on each open $V \subseteq X$ by the colimit $\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$) is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} $\struct{S}(U)$ for an arbitrary affine open $U \supseteq f V$. The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$ for $A = \struct{S}(U)$, $B = \struct{X}(V)$ on an affine chart $V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U$ with $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$ affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$ is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$ in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$, not for $\struct{S}(U)$. Transferring to the classical $A = \struct{S}(U)$ source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exist an affine open $U \subseteq S$, an affine open $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)$, such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure $\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)$ induced by $f$) is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix $x \in X$. We use the abbreviation $A := \Gamma(S, U)$ and $B := \Gamma(X, V)$ for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map $A \to B$ is the appLE map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}$ induced by $f$.
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \leq f^{-1}U$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ side condition.} The chart witness $x \in V$ shows that the open $V \subseteq X$ is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring $B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)$ is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart $(U, V, e)$ produced by Step 1 satisfies $x \in V$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V$, and under the appLE algebra structure $A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)$, the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
104	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
105	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
106	\end{remark}

108	\section{Bridge: presheaf form $\leftrightarrow$ algebra-K\"ahler form on an affine chart (milestone M1)}
109	\label{sec:bridge}
110	
111	Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} is stated in the \emph{algebra-K\"ahler form} $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) / \Gamma(S, U)}$ for an affine chart $V \subseteq f^{-1}U$, while the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ of Definition~\ref{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf} carries on its section over $V$ the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ over the presheaf-level inverse-image colimit ring $A_{\mathrm{colim}} = (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$. On a single affine chart the two are connected by a canonical $B$-linear isomorphism whose construction is the content of milestone~M1 of the project roadmap (\texttt{STRATEGY.md}).
112	
113	\begin{theorem}[Bridge between presheaf and algebra-K\"ahler forms]
114	  \label{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE}
115	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE}
116	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj, lem:appLE_isLocalization}
117	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes, let $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ be affine opens with $V \leq f^{-1}U$, and write $A := \Gamma(S, U)$, $B := \Gamma(X, V)$, with the appLE algebra structure $A \to B$ induced by $f$. There is a canonical $B$-linear equivalence
118	  \[
119	    (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(V)
120	    \;\simeq_{B}\;
121	    \Omega_{B \,/\, A}
122	  \]
123	  identifying the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf on $V$ with the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential module of Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}. The Lean target packages this as a \texttt{LinearEquiv} (\texttt{≃ₗ[B]}), mirroring Mathlib's convention for \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale}.
124	\end{theorem}
125	
126	\begin{proof}
127	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj, lem:appLE_isLocalization, lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton, lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
128	  The proof decomposes into the four sub-steps M1.a--M1.e of the roadmap. We use the abbreviation $A_{\mathrm{colim}} := (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ for the colimit ring carrying the presheaf-side K\"ahler module.
129	
130	  \emph{M1.a: the multiplicative set of ``good'' elements.} Define
131	  \[
132	    M \;:=\; \{\, g \in A \,:\, (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times} \,\}
133	  \]
134	  as the set of elements of $A$ whose image in $B$ is a unit. $M$ is a submonoid: $1 \in M$ (the appLE map is a ring homomorphism, so $\mathrm{appLE}(1) = 1$, a unit), and if $g, h \in M$ then $\mathrm{appLE}(gh) = \mathrm{appLE}(g)\,\mathrm{appLE}(h) \in B^{\times}$ as the product of two units.
135	
136	  \emph{M1.b: $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is the localization at $M$.} This is the heart of the bridge and is recorded as Lemma~\ref{lem:appLE_isLocalization} below. The argument re-frames the colimit comparison as a direct \texttt{IsLocalization} check via Mathlib's \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le}: build the two ring homomorphisms $A_M \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ (via the universal property of \texttt{Localization}~$M$, after observing that each $g \in M$ is already a unit in $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$) and $A_{\mathrm{colim}} \to A_M$ (via the cocone universal property, using basic-open refinements $D(g) \subseteq W$ for each open $W \supseteq f V$ to factor through some $A_g$), then verify the composites are identities via \texttt{IsLocalization.ringHom\_ext}, and conclude \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ via \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le}. This pattern avoids the \texttt{Functor.Final} colim-comparison framing entirely (Mathlib has no off-the-shelf ``colim of localizations is localization at the union submonoid'' lemma; the analogist-verified pattern is the two-direction \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le} construction). The single-element-localization fact $\struct{S}(D(g)) = A_g$ is supplied by Mathlib's \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen}.
137	
138	  \emph{M1.c: the K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial.} Mathlib already supplies this: by \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}), the algebra map $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is \texttt{FormallyUnramified}; and the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential} (same file) then gives $\Subsingleton\,\Omega_{A_{\mathrm{colim}} / A}$. The project-side Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton} re-exports this conclusion in two lines (no genuine Mathlib gap-fill is needed here, contra an earlier framing of M1.c).
139	
140	  \emph{M1.d: the surjection $\Omega_{B/A} \twoheadrightarrow \Omega_{B/A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ is an iso.} Apply Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso} below (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map} in Mathlib \texttt{RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler}, plus the M1.c conclusion that $\Omega_{A_{\mathrm{colim}}/A} = 0$). The second fundamental exact sequence
141	  \[
142	    B \otimes_{A_{\mathrm{colim}}} \Omega_{A_{\mathrm{colim}} / A}
143	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B / A}
144	    \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}
145	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
146	  \]
147	  has zero left-hand term by M1.c, so the right-hand surjection is an iso.
148	
149	  \emph{M1.e: combine with the $\mathrm{rfl}$-identification of the presheaf section.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, $(\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(V) = \Omega_{B / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ definitionally. Composing the rfl-equality with the inverse of the M1.d iso gives the claimed $B$-linear isomorphism.
150	\end{proof}
151	
152	\begin{lemma}[$A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is a localization at $M$]
153	  \label{lem:appLE_isLocalization}
154	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.appLE_isLocalization}
155	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
156	  In the setting of Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE}, the canonical ring homomorphism $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M := \{g \in A : (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}\}$.
157	\end{lemma}
158	
159	\begin{proof}
160	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
161	  We construct the \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ predicate directly via the two-direction \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le} pattern, avoiding any \texttt{Functor.Final} colim-comparison (Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} has no off-the-shelf ``colim of localizations is localization at the union submonoid'' lemma, so this pattern is the canonical project-local route).
162	
163	  \emph{Step 0: each $g \in M$ is a unit in $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} Fix $g \in M$, so $\mathrm{appLE}(g) \in B^{\times}$. The basic open $D(g) \subseteq U$ contains $f V$ (by the bridge identity $f V \subseteq D(g) \iff \mathrm{appLE}(g) \in B^{\times}$, following from \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}'s \texttt{IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_eq\_iff\_isUnit} together with $D(g) = f^{-1}\,U.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g$ on the affine source side). The cocone leg of the directed colimit corresponding to the open $D(g)$ produces a ring map $\struct{S}(D(g)) \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$, and on $D(g)$ itself the element $g$ is invertible (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen} supplies the \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$\struct{S}(D(g))$ instance, so $g$ is a unit in $\struct{S}(D(g))$). Pushing this unit through the cocone leg shows $g$ is a unit in $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.
164	
165	  \emph{Step 1: the algebra map $A_M \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} The universal property of \texttt{Localization}~$M$ (\texttt{IsLocalization.lift}, \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic}) applied to the algebra map $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ — together with Step 0 — produces a ring homomorphism $\varphi \colon A_M \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ over $A$.
166	
167	  \emph{Step 2: the algebra map $A_{\mathrm{colim}} \to A_M$.} The colimit $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ has the universal property of the cocone over $\{\struct{S}(W) : f V \subseteq W \subseteq S\}$; to produce a ring map out of $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ it suffices to specify a compatible family of ring maps from each cocone node. For each open $W \supseteq f V$, restrict to $W \subseteq U$ (intersecting with $U$ preserves the cocone since $f V \subseteq U$), cover $W \cap U$ by basic opens $D(g_i) \subseteq U$ (\texttt{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens}), use quasi-compactness of $f V$ to extract a finite sub-cover, take $g := \prod g_i$ so that $D(g) \subseteq W \cap U$ and $f V \subseteq D(g)$ (i.e.\ $g \in M$); then $\struct{S}(W) \to \struct{S}(D(g)) = A_g \to A_M$ via the canonical $A_g \to A_M$ supplied by the inclusion of submonoids $\langle g \rangle \subseteq M$. Compatibility on intersections follows from the universal property of $A_g$ at each level.
168	
169	  \emph{Step 3: composites are identities.} \texttt{IsLocalization.ringHom\_ext} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic}) shows that any two ring homomorphisms $A_M \to R$ agreeing on the image of $A$ are equal. The composite $A_M \to A_{\mathrm{colim}} \to A_M$ agrees with the identity on $A$ (both send $a \mapsto a/1$), hence is the identity. Symmetrically, any two ring homomorphisms $A_{\mathrm{colim}} \to R$ agreeing on every cocone node $\struct{S}(W)$ are equal; the composite $A_{\mathrm{colim}} \to A_M \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ agrees with the identity on every $\struct{S}(D(g))$ for $g \in M$ (both factor through the canonical $\struct{S}(D(g)) \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$), and basic opens with $g \in M$ form a cofinal subfamily of opens containing $f V$, so the composite is the identity on the whole colimit.
170	
171	  \emph{Step 4: conclude \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} The isomorphism $A_M \cong A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ over $A$ — together with the canonical \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_M$ instance for \texttt{Localization}~$M$ — implies \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ via \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Defs}), more directly via \texttt{IsLocalization.isLocalization\_of\_algEquiv} or \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_ringEquiv}.
172	
173	  \emph{Mathlib leverage summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces are
174	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen} (Steps 0, 2),
175	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_eq\_iff\_isUnit} (Step 0 bridge identity),
176	  \texttt{IsLocalization.lift} (Step 1),
177	  \texttt{IsLocalization.ringHom\_ext} (Step 3), and
178	  \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le} or \texttt{IsLocalization.isLocalization\_of\_algEquiv} (Step 4).
179	  The cocone leg-morphisms of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} are verified to exist in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227}.
180	
181	  \emph{Cross-chapter parallel.} A similar two-direction \texttt{IsLocalization.of\_le} pattern can be used elsewhere in the project whenever a colimit of localizations needs to be identified with a localization at the union submonoid.
182	
183	  The Mathlib analogist's persistent file \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md} (iter-121) records the full design rationale for the chosen API shape.
184	\end{proof}
185	
186	\begin{lemma}[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
187	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
188	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
189	  \uses{lem:appLE_isLocalization}
190	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$. Then $\Omega_{L / A}$ is subsingleton (the zero $L$-module).
191	\end{lemma}
192	
193	\begin{proof}
194	  This is a thin re-export of Mathlib's existing infrastructure: \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}, line 303) produces the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified}~$A$~$L$ from any \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$L$ predicate; the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}~\texttt{[verified]} (same file, lines 57--59) then yields \texttt{Subsingleton}~$(\mathrm{KaehlerDifferential}~A~L)$. Hence the project-side lemma is a two-line re-export ($\mathtt{haveI} := \mathtt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~M$ \texttt{; infer\_instance}); contra an earlier framing, no Mathlib gap-fill is required for this lemma.
195	\end{proof}
196	
197	\begin{lemma}[Tower-cancellation equivalence for K\"ahler modules under localization]
198	  \label{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
199	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
200	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
201	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization (in the sense of \texttt{IsLocalization}), and let $L \to B$ be a ring map forming a scalar tower $A \to L \to B$. Then the canonical $B$-linear map $\Omega_{B/A} \to \Omega_{B/L}$ induced by the tower is a $B$-linear equivalence.
202	\end{lemma}
203	
204	\begin{proof}
205	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
206	  The second fundamental exact sequence of K\"ahler differentials reads
207	  \[
208	    B \otimes_{L} \Omega_{L/A} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/A} \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/L} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
209	  \]
210	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}, the left term vanishes (since $\Omega_{L/A}$ is subsingleton and tensoring against it gives the zero module). Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel and is a $B$-linear equivalence. The Mathlib closure pieces are \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map}~\texttt{[verified]} (the exact sequence above) and \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.map\_surjective}~\texttt{[verified]} (the right-hand surjection), both in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}; the project-side declaration packages these into a \texttt{LinearEquiv} via \texttt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}. This is the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case, candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}.
211	\end{proof}
212	
213	\begin{remark}[Mathlib contribution candidate]
214	  \label{rem:bridge_mathlib_pr}
215	  Per the analogist's analysis (\texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}, iter-121), the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1 is Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case (candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}, candidate Mathlib home \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}). Lemma~\ref{lem:appLE_isLocalization} is more scheme-morphism-shaped and is best kept project-local; if a future Mathlib PR wants it, extraction from \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme} is straightforward.
216	\end{remark}

218	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
219	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
220	
221	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
222	
223	The reverse implication
224	\[
225	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
226	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
227	\]
228	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
229	
230	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
231	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
232	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
233	\end{remark}
234	
235	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
236	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
237	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
238	  \[
239	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
240	  \]
241	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
242	  \begin{itemize}
243	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
244	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
245	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
246	  \end{itemize}
247	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
248	\end{remark}
249	
250	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
251	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
252	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
253	\end{remark}
254	
255	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
256	
257	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
258	
259	\begin{itemize}
260	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
261	
262	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
263	  \[
264	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
265	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
266	  \]
267	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
268	
269	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
270	
271	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
272	  \[
273	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
274	  \]
275	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
276	\end{itemize}

353	If `f : X ⟶ Y` is an open map and `ℱ` is a presheaf on `Y`, then the pullback of `ℱ` by `f` is

355	-/
356	@[simps!]
357	def pullbackObjIso {X Y : TopCat.{v}} {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : IsOpenMap f) (ℱ : Y.Presheaf C) :
358	    (pullback C f).obj ℱ ≅ hf.functor.op ⋙ ℱ :=
359	  NatIso.ofComponents
360	    (fun U ↦ pullbackObjObjOfImageOpen f ℱ U.1 (hf (unop U).1 (unop U).2))
361	    (fun {U V} i ↦ (pullbackObjObjOfImageOpen_hom_naturality f ℱ (hf (unop V).1 (unop V).2)
362	      (hf (unop U).1 (unop U).2) (leOfHom i.unop)))

1	/-

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
69	
70	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
71	
72	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
73	
74	/-- The submonoid of "good" elements in `Γ(S, U)`: those whose image under the
75	appLE algebra map `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` induced by `f : X ⟶ S` is a unit in
76	`Γ(X, V)`. This is the multiplicative set at which `Γ(S, U) → A_colim` is a
77	localization (cf. `appLE_isLocalization`). -/
78	def appLE_unitSubmonoid (f : X ⟶ S)
79	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
80	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Submonoid Γ(S, U) where
81	  carrier := { g | IsUnit ((Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom g) }
82	  one_mem' := by
83	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_one]
84	    exact isUnit_one
85	  mul_mem' := by
86	    intro a b ha hb
87	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_mul] at ha hb ⊢
88	    exact ha.mul hb
89	
90	/-- The inverse-image presheaf colimit ring at `V` is the localization
91	of `Γ(S, U)` at the submonoid `appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e`.
92	
93	This is milestone M1.b of the project roadmap: the heart of the bridge between
94	the section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` and the appLE-algebra
95	Kähler module on an affine chart. The proof goes via the two-direction
96	`IsLocalization.of_le` pattern (see
97	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge`).
98	
99	The canonical `Algebra Γ(S, U) A_colim` structure is the cocone leg of
100	the directed colimit at the open `U` (valid since `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` means
101	`fV ⊆ U`, so `U` is in the index category of the colimit). Constructing
102	this algebra structure rigorously is part of the prover's M1.b task; in
103	the stub we introduce it via a `sorry`-ed `letI`. -/
104	theorem appLE_isLocalization (f : X ⟶ S)
105	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
106	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
107	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U)
108	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) :=
109	      sorry
110	    IsLocalization (appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e)
111	      (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) := by
112	  sorry
113	
114	end AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
115	
116	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
117	
118	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
119	
120	/-- The bridge between the presheaf form and the algebra-Kähler form of the
121	relative cotangent module on an affine chart (milestone M1 of the project
122	roadmap). The section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over an
123	affine `V ⊆ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` is `Γ(X, V)`-linearly equivalent to the appLE-algebra
124	Kähler module `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)`.
125	
126	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge` for the proof
127	strategy (M1.a–M1.e).
128	
129	The `Module Γ(X, V)` instance on the LHS section module is supplied by the
130	`PresheafOfModules` structure (via the underlying
131	`(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).obj (.op V) : ModuleCat _`); in the
132	stub we introduce it via a `sorry`-ed `letI` so the type checks, and the
133	prover will replace this with the canonical transport-of-instance when
134	they refine the construction. -/
135	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE
136	    (f : X ⟶ S)
137	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
138	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
139	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
140	      (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
141	    letI : Module Γ(X, V) ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)) :=
142	      sorry
143	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)) ≃ₗ[Γ(X, V)]
144	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e) := by
145	  sorry
146	
147	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
148	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
149	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
150	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
151	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
152	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
153	
154	The bridge from this algebra-Kähler form to the project's presheaf
155	form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)` is a
156	Mathlib gap: the section module of the presheaf identifies with the
157	Kähler module over the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring
158	`colim_{f V ⊆ W ⊆ S} Γ(S, W)`, which is a localization of `Γ(S, U)`
159	and hence yields an iso `Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ≃ Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ A_colim]`
160	via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`; constructing the iso
161	requires presheaf-level cofinality machinery (~200–400 LOC) that is
162	out of autonomous-loop scope. See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`
163	§ "Bridge to the relative cotangent presheaf — out of autonomous-loop
164	scope" for the mathematical content.
165	
166	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
167	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
168	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
169	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
170	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
171	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
172	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
173	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
174	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
175	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
176	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
177	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
178	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
179	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
180	  intro x
181	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
182	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
183	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
184	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
185	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
186	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
187	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
188	      first
189	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
190	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
191	
192	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
